name: Object Pool
def: Reuses a fixed set of pre-allocated objects instead of repeatedly allocating and freeing.
problem: Avoid allocation churn and GC pressure for expensive or high-frequency short-lived objects.
mechanism: A free list hands out idle objects and reclaims them on release; objects are reset between uses rather than reallocated.
benefits: Eliminates alloc/GC churn in hot loops
limits: Stale state if objects not reset
Pool sizing is a tuning problem
failures: Leaked objects never returned to the pool
whenNot: Cheap objects (pooling adds complexity for no gain)
When GC/alloc is already not a bottleneck
myths: "Pooling always helps" — modern allocators/GCs often beat naive pools
